mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, enabling state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for deploying large language models in production environments, offering features like intelligent scheduling and workload autoscaling. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for compatible hardware accelerators.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d CLI tool, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This installs the CLI tool on a Linux system.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This ensures kubectl can communicate with your cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This command installs the llm-d application using Helm, deploying it in the 'llm-d' namespace.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

kubectl create namespace llm-d

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --namespace llm-d --version 0.5.1

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Creates a namespace for llm-d.
> Installs llm-d in the specified namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies autoscaling configuration.
> Sets up monitoring for llm-d.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d application.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --namespace llm-d --version 0.5.1

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d

```

> Check the status of the pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get services' and check the deployment logs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
